task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window is open displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/gull_wing_spec.txt.

task_completion.description: Create a new Wing geometry in OpenVSP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP application is open with a default Wing geometry visible in the 3D workspace or listed in the Geometry Browser.

task_completion.description: Modify the wing topology to have two spanwise panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP parameter window (Section tab) shows multiple sections (e.g., Section 1 and Section 2), or the 3D wing model clearly displays a split/joint indicating an inner and outer panel.

task_completion.description: Apply the specified anhedral angle to the inboard section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The inner section of the wing in the 3D view is angled downwards towards the ground, or the Dihedral parameter for the inner section is set to a negative value (e.g., -15.0) in the parameter window.

task_completion.description: Apply the specified dihedral angle to the outboard section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outer section of the wing in the 3D view is angled upwards, creating the classic 'inverted gull wing' shape, or the Dihedral parameter for the outer section is set to a positive value (e.g., 8.5) in the parameter window.

task_completion.description: Save the completed model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or the OpenVSP main window title bar shows the file being saved as 'inverted_gull_wing.vsp3' in the '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenVSP graphical user interface to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenVSP GUI being actively used, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor writing XML/VSP3 code.

integrity.description: The 3D model is genuinely rendered in the workspace.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP 3D view displays the rendered wing geometry updating as parameters are changed, confirming actual software interaction.

integrity.description: The agent manually adjusted the wing parameters.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the OpenVSP parameter dialogs (e.g., 'Geom' window, 'Plan' or 'Section' tabs) to change angles and spans, rather than just downloading or copying a pre-made file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file path is /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/inverted_gull_wing.vsp3. The target half span is 6.3m (inboard 2.5m + outboard 3.8m). The inboard section should have an anhedral angle of -15.0 degrees (pointing down). The outboard section should have a dihedral angle of +8.5 degrees (pointing up).
